allow: Bash(git -C "C:\\Harbor\\FurpectSync-Prototype" diff HEAD~1 HEAD -- Assets/Scenes/Ragdoll.unity | head -200)

Bash(python3 << 'EOF'
# Parse the DynamicsManager layer collision matrix
matrix_hex = "ffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffff3fffffffbfffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffff"

# Convert to binary - each byte represents 8 layers
# The matrix is 32 bytes \(256 bits for 32 layers, but stored as 256 bits\)
# Each 4 bits \(4 chars in hex\) represents interaction between one layer and 8 layers

# Better approach: convert hex string to binary and analyze specific bits
binary = bin\(int\(matrix_hex, 16\)\)[2:].zfill\(256\)

# Layer 6 is bit 6, Layer 7 is bit 7
# The collision matrix is stored as: bit \(layer1 * 32 + layer2\) tells if they collide

layer6_index = 6 * 32 + 7  # Layer 6 colliding with Layer 7
layer7_index = 7 * 32 + 6  # Layer 7 colliding with Layer 6

# Note: binary string is reversed compared to bit position
bit6to7 = binary[255 - layer6_index] if layer6_index < 256 else '0'
bit7to6 = binary[255 - layer7_index] if layer7_index < 256 else '0'

print\(f"Layer collision matrix analysis:"\)
print\(f"Layer 6 -> Layer 7 collision: {bit6to7}"\)
print\(f"Layer 7 -> Layer 6 collision: {bit7to6}"\)

# Also check individual layers against all
for check_layer in [6, 7]:
    print\(f"\\nLayer {check_layer} collisions:"\)
    for other_layer in range\(8\):
        bit_index = check_layer * 32 + other_layer
        if bit_index < 256:
            bit_value = binary[255 - bit_index]
            print\(f"  Layer {check_layer} <-> Layer {other_layer}: {bit_value}"\)
EOF)
